description_long: This is a robust, double-handled terracotta vase with a wide, open mouth and a bulbous, ribbed body. The surface is matte and shows signs of age, including discoloration, surface abrasions, and a few visible chips. The handles are thick and arch gracefully from the rim to the shoulder, providing both structural support and a decorative element. The coloration ranges from warm, earthy orange-browns to darker, weathered patches, suggesting exposure to the elements or long-term use. The rim is slightly uneven, and the body features subtle vertical ridges that add texture and visual interest. The interior appears hollow and unfinished, typical of utilitarian pottery.
description: A weathered, double-handled terracotta vase with a ribbed, bulbous body and matte, earthy surface. The vase shows signs of age and use, with visible chips and discoloration.
description_view_0: Front view showing both handles, the wide mouth, and the ribbed, rounded body with visible surface wear.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting one handle in profile and the curvature of the body, with more pronounced surface texture and darker discoloration.
description_view_2: Rear view with both handles visible, showing the darker, more weathered side of the vase and the full extent of the ribbed body.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the other handle and the lighter, less weathered surface, with visible chips and abrasions.
